title: centaurea affinis subsp. chalcidicea
intro: Centaurea affinis subsp. chalcidicea, also known as a type of knapweed, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea affinis subsp. chalcidicea include relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its flowers and leaves, although its use is not widely documented and should be approached with caution.
